data.title: De Beers draws interest from ex-CEOs as Anglo American starts sale - Bloomberg
data.description:
Anglo American (OTCQX:AAUKD) (OTCQX:NGLOD) is set to begin the formal sales process for its De Beers unit after it received interest from potential buyers including two former CEOs of the diamond company, Bloomberg reported Thursday.

Former De Beers CEOs Gareth Penny and Bruce Cleaver are both leading groups that are potential buyers, according to the report, which added that the company also has attracted attention from Australian mining veteran Michael O'Keeffe, according to the report.

The De Beers sale is the final step in Anglo's (OTCQX:AAUKD) (OTCQX:NGLOD) sweeping restructuring plan aimed at refocusing on core assets like copper and iron ore; the company is also weighing options for an IPO or demerger of De Beers, but a sale is the preferred and most likely option, the report said.

Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKD) (OTCQX:NGLOD) has waited to sell De Beers as the diamond industry faced a crisis after a collapse in Chinese demand and fierce competition from synthetic diamonds; Anglo wrote down the value of the unit for a second time in February, to ~$4.9B.

data.title: Ryan Specialty to acquire Michigan-based J.M. Wilson
data.description:
Specialty insurer Ryan Specialty Holdings (NYSE:RYAN) announced on Thursday the acquisition of Michigan-based J.M. Wilson.

The operations of J.M. Wilson are expected to become a part of RT Binding Authority, the binding authority specialty of Ryan.

The terms of the deal were not disclosed.

The transaction is expected to close in the third quarter.

data.title: Palantir CEO denies NYT report on data collection: report
data.description:
During an interview today on CNBC, Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR) CEO Alex Karp denied allegations from a New York Times article last week that said his company was "tapped" by the Trump Administration to "compile a master list of personal information on Americans." 

Karp said the company is "not surveilling Americans," when asked about the article. 

This follows up a denial by Palantir in a post on X dated June 3.

"The recently published article by the New York Times is blatantly untrue," the post reads. "Palantir never collects data to unlawfully surveil Americans, and our Foundry platform employs granular security protections. If the facts were on its side, the New York Times would not have needed to twist the truth."

The New York Times said the Denver-based Palantir might be the company tapped to handle U.S. President Donald Trump's executive order that called for the federal government to merge information and data across its agencies. The claim was made as Palantir continues to secure government contracts.

During the interview with CNBC, Karp also highlighted the importance of the U.S. dominating the artificial intelligence race.

"My general bias on AI is it is dangerous," Karp said. "There are positive and negative consequences, and either we win or China will win."

"There is no economy in the world with this kind of corporate leadership which is willing to pivot, which understands technologies, which is willing to look at new things, but also has deep domain expertise," he said. "Our allies in the West, in Europe, are going to have to learn from us."

data.title: Fed's Kugler says current restrictive monetary policy stance remains appropriate
data.description:
Federal Reserve Governor Adriana Kugler supports the central bank maintaining its policy rate at its current level if risks for higher inflation continue, she said on Thursday.

"I see greater upside risks to inflation at this juncture and potential downside risks to employment and output growth down the road, and this leads me to continue to support maintaining the FOMC's policy rate at its current setting if upside risks to inflation remain," she said in a speech at the Economic Club of New York.

"I view our current stance of monetary policy as well positioned for any changes in the macroeconomic environment," she added. The federal funds rate target range is currently at 4.25%-4.50%.

Nontraditional data on economic data appear to be consistent with her overall assessment that the U.S. economic growth may moderate, but hasn't yet experienced a significant slowdown.

Specifically, longer-run inflation expectations have stayed relatively stable.

Ahead of the May employment report that will be issued on Friday, "labor supply and demand  remain in relative balance," she noted.  On the inflation side, of the Fed's dual-mandate, core PCE inflation stood at 2.5% in April, still 0.5 percentage point above the Fed's target. "So I do believe that our monetary policy stance, which I view as modestly restrictive, is currently appropriate to achieve and sustain 2%," Kugler said.

Developing...check back for updates.

data.title: Mortgage rates fall after three consecutive weeks of increase
data.description:
Mortgage rates declined this week after three straight weeks of increase, the Freddie Mac Primary Mortgage Survey showed.

30-year fixed-rate mortgage averaged 6.85% as of June 5, down from 6.89% last week and 6.99% in the year-ago week.

15-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 5.99%, down from 6.03% a week ago and 6.29% a year ago.

"The average mortgage rate decreased this week, which is welcome news to potential homebuyers who also are seeing inventory improve and house price growth slow," said Sam Khater, Freddie Mac's chief economist.

data.title: Duluth outlines $15M annualized expense reduction and 20% SKU cut by spring 2026 amid turnaround push
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Duluth Holdings Inc. (DLTH) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Stephanie L. Pugliese, President and CEO, returned to lead Duluth and emphasized the company's unique brand identity and its strong customer loyalty, noting her commitment to restoring financial health and achieving profitable growth. She stated, "I am leading with a sense of urgency, clarifying direction and taking action. Our operating model and processes need to be simplified quickly to restore financial health in the business. We will then position Duluth for long-term profitable growth."
  • Pugliese introduced three key focus areas: reinvigorating brand awareness, prioritizing solution-based product innovation with a more focused assortment and a 20% SKU reduction by spring 2026, and leveraging the omnichannel model to enhance customer service. She detailed, "We will narrow our assortment breadth with at least a 20% reduction of SKUs by spring 2026, while innovating in growth areas, including core men's and women's workwear, and adjacencies such as first layer and outdoor."
  • She announced an expense savings initiative to deliver approximately $15 million in annualized savings, with at least $10 million expected in the current fiscal year, aiming to "reduce complexity in the business and increase our focus on innovation and our brand enablers."
  • The company is pursuing direct-to-factory sourcing to reduce costs and bring product innovations to market faster, continuing to optimize its fulfillment network, and rigorously evaluating real estate with one store closure and two new stores planned for fall.
  • Heena K. Agrawal, CFO, stated, "In 2025, our key priorities are to reset promotions, restore price integrity, improve inventory management and strengthen operational execution."

Outlook

  • Duluth is maintaining its fiscal year 2025 adjusted EBITDA guidance in the $20 million to $25 million range, considering cost mitigation efforts, ongoing expense reductions, and macroeconomic uncertainty.
  • Management expects to offset tariff impacts through targeted price increases, vendor negotiations, and managing future inventory receipts.
  • The company is on track to reduce apparel SKUs by more than 5% in fall 2025 and over 20% by spring/summer 2026, with further reductions in non-apparel hardgoods beginning fall 2025.
  • Inventory levels are anticipated to normalize in the second half of the year, with year-end inventory projected to be down double digits compared to the prior year. Capital expenditures are reduced by $3 million to approximately $17 million, focusing on priority investments.

Financial Results

  • Reported Q1 2025 net sales were $102.7 million, a decline of 12% year-over-year. Reported EPS loss was ($0.45), with an adjusted EPS loss of ($0.32). Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was minus $3.8 million.
  • Direct channel sales, excluding wholesale, fell 14.6%. Retail store sales declined 2.6% while showing improved shopper conversion.
  • Gross profit margin rate declined by 80 basis points year-over-year, though March and April combined gross margin improved by over 300 basis points versus last year.
  • SG&A spend was $65.7 million (adjusted $65.2 million), $5.4 million lower than last year, with deleverage due to lower sales and higher shipping costs. Cash and cash equivalents ended at $8.6 million, with borrowings of $64 million and net liquidity of $45 million at quarter end.
  • Inventory was $176.1 million, up $39.7 million year-over-year, with 91% in current inventory mix and 9% clearance.
  • In April, Duluth transitioned its credit facility to a $100 million asset-based lending agreement extending through 2030.

Q&A

  • Janine Marie Hoffman Stichter, BTIG: Asked about brand awareness investments and potential reinvestment of savings into marketing. Pugliese responded that the company is evaluating and seeing initial success with upper funnel marketing, aiming to drive both awareness and visits, and will shift dollars as returns improve.
  • Stichter: Inquired about consumer response to promotional reduction and expectations for price increases. Pugliese explained that sequential improvement in gross margin began in March and April, and that promotional pullbacks are balanced with customer retention strategies. She noted price increases are targeted to unique products with pricing elasticity, maintaining a value proposition for customers. Agrawal added, "We are seeing a lot of green shoots, especially as we reduce the depth of the offers. We are seeing positive trends in conversion, in higher full price sales and especially on our store channel."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone was neutral to slightly positive, focusing on execution and brand awareness strategy, without pressing on negative issues or skepticism.
  • Management projected confidence during prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing urgency and decisive action, with statements such as "I am leading with a sense of urgency, clarifying direction and taking action."
  • Compared to last quarter, management’s tone shifted from addressing operational setbacks and leadership transition to a more focused and urgent turnaround narrative.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company’s leadership changed from Sam Sato to Stephanie Pugliese, with a renewed strategic focus and immediate cost actions in Q1 2025.
  • Compared to Q4 2024, the current quarter saw sharper top-line declines and a more urgent approach to expense reduction, SKU rationalization, and marketing adjustments.
  • Previous quarter focused on logistics and fulfillment challenges, while the current quarter emphasized operational simplification and growth initiatives.
  • Analysts' questions shifted from operations and fulfillment to brand awareness, promotional strategies, and marketing investment.
  • Management sentiment is more urgent and action-oriented versus the previous quarter’s transitional and reflective tone.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited continued macroeconomic uncertainty and potential top-line headwinds.
  • Tariff increases are expected to add $14 million in product costs, with mitigation through targeted price increases, vendor negotiations, and managed receipts.
  • Elevated inventory levels and clearance mix were noted, with normalization expected in the second half.
  • The company is actively addressing expense structure and fulfillment network optimization to protect profitability.

Final Takeaway

Duluth Holdings is responding to a challenging retail environment with decisive cost reductions, a renewed brand focus, and a significant SKU rationalization effort. Management remains committed to simplifying operations, restoring profitability, and leveraging both existing and new leadership to drive future growth, while actively managing risks from tariffs and inventory levels.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: ReconAfrica upsizes underwritten offering to C$16.5M
data.description:
• Reconnaissance Energy Africa (TSXV:RECO:CA) has increased the size of its public offering, pursuant to which the underwriters have agreed to purchase 33M units at a price of C$0.50‎ per unit for aggregate gross proceeds of C$16.5M.
  • In connection with the offering, indications of lead orders have been received from BW Energy (OTCPK:BWEFF), directors and management members of ReconAfrica, and certain other investors, expecting to purchase over C$4M of units.
  • BW Energy has agreed to a follow-on strategic equity investment in the company of around C$2M under the offering, reaffirming it's previously announced strategic partnership with ReconAfrica.
  • The net proceeds from the offering will be used for exploration activities, working capital and general corporate purposes. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Temu and SHEIN traffic slumps under weight of U.S. trade policies
data.description:
The end of the de minimis exemption is undermining traffic to two of China’s most popular e-commerce platforms, industry data has shown.

According to data shared from Sensor Tower to CNBC, fast-fashion retailer SHEIN and e-commerce marketplace Temu (NASDAQ:PDD) suffered a 25% and 52% drop in U.S. daily active users (“DAU”), respectively, corresponding to an elimination of the de minimis exemption and reduction in U.S. advertising spending by both platforms.

Monthly average user growth (“MAU”) in the U.S. also eroded by 30% for Temu (NASDAQ:PDD) and by 12% for SHEIN in May versus March.

The de minimis exemption allowed packages with contents valued at or less than $800 to enter the U.S. without tariffs. The exemption was intended to simplify the customs practice for low-value packages, but was instead exploited by companies like SHEIN and Temu (NASDAQ:PDD) to avoid paying import taxes by shipping merchandise directly from sellers in China rather than establish warehouses in the U.S. In April, the Trump Administration closed the loophole, particularly those imports from China, and initially subjected all relevant postal items to a 120% tax. A subsequent executive order from the White House in May lowered the de minimis tariff to 54%.

“Temu and SHEIN’s decline in US ad spend was also noticeable in April, as spending decreased by 40% and 65% YoY, respectively,” Seema Shah, vice president of research and insights at Sensor Tower, said in emailed comments to CNBC.

The loss of DAUs and MAUs was reflected in Apple App store rankings with Temu dropping to 132 in May from the top 3 last year and SHEIN falling to 60 from the top 10.

In the company’s most recent quarterly results, PDD’s (PDD) VP of finance acknowledged the detrimental impact U.S. trade policy is having on the company’s economic well-being.

“A slowdown in growth rate is expected as our business scales and challenges emerge,” PDD’s Jun Liu said, adding that the company’s financial results “may continue to reflect the impact of sustained investments in the ecosystem as we support merchants and consumers through uncertain times.”

As part of a strategy to reduce shipping costs, Temu (PDD) has expanded its marketplace to include U.S.-based merchants who can manage their own inventory, and efforts to localize fulfillment operations with the inclusion of U.S. warehouses.

SHEIN is also increasing its onshoring efforts with warehouses in Indiana, Texas, New Jersey and two in California with offices to support logistics in Seattle and Los Angeles.

data.title: Ballard Power wins fuel cell engine order from Sierra Northern Railway
data.description:
Ballard Power Systems (NASDAQ:BLDP) +2.8% in Thursday's trading after saying it signed a new supply agreement with Sierra Northern Railway for 1.5 MW of fuel cell engines to be delivered before the end of this year; financial details were not disclosed.

Ballard (NASDAQ:BLDP) said it will deliver 12 FCmove-XD fuel cell engines to convert three diesel locomotives into hydrogen-powered, zero-emission units; the FCmove-XD modules are engineered specifically for heavy-duty uses, providing high performance in terms of reliability, durability, efficiency and power density while also providing zero emissions without the need for the installation of expensive overhead catenary systems.

"This initiative not only enhances our operational efficiency but is also a pivotal step in California's efforts to reduce greenhouse gas emissions in the transportation sector," Sierra Northern Railway President Randy MacEwen said.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow rise after Trump holds phone call with China's Xi
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages pushed higher on Thursday, after U.S. President Donald Trump held what he called a "very good phone call" with Chinese President Xi Jinping.

The S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.4% in morning trade, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.7%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.3%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was up 4 basis points to 4.39%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) advanced 4 basis points to 3.92%.

Trump on Truth Social said the call "resulted in a very positive conclusion" for the U.S. and China. Trump said there "should no longer be any questions" over rare earths, and that he had been invited by Xi to visit China. 

The U.S. trade deficit narrowed sharply in April as imports plunged.

The initial jobless claims rose more than expected in the past week. 

Productivity growth was revised lower in the final Q1 estimates, and labor costs extended their climb.

The U.S. services sector leads in May job cuts, according to the Challenger Report.

Wall Street on Wednesday closed little changed, while bonds rallied after private hiring in May reached its lowest level in over two years.

"Markets put in a strong performance yesterday, after a weak batch of US data led to a massive rally for U.S.Treasuries, which in turn supported risk assets," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

Of course, even with the rally, the data still raised fears that the U.S. economy lost steam into May. That started off with the ADP’s report of private payrolls, which was the softest in over two years, Allen added. 

The main event in terms of labor data—the monthly employment reading—will land on Friday.
